title: Rolling Potting Island
summary: A cedar-topped outdoor potting bench on 4x4 legs with a slatted bottom shelf and casters, sized for real gardening work.
description:
Four 4x4 legs, cut short for real outdoor stability, carry a 2x4 frame — sides, front/back, and a bottom support tying everything together at floor-shelf height. Build the frame square before adding either the top or bottom shelf boards, since a potting island holding bags of soil and pots needs to sit level to be useful.

Cedar 1x6 boards (or fence pickets, which work just as well and cost less) form both the bottom shelf and the top work surface — cedar's natural rot resistance matters here since this piece lives outside and gets dirty and wet constantly. Space the boards with small consistent gaps so soil and water can fall through rather than pooling.

**Casters are optional but recommended** — mount them to the bottom of the legs, adjusting leg length to compensate for the wheel height, so the whole island can be rolled to wherever potting work is happening that day.

materials.name: 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)

cutList.note: Cut shorter to compensate for caster wheel height if using.

cutList.material: 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)
1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 35-1/2" from the 4x4. Cut eight sides and top supports at 22", four front and back at 48" and one bottom support at 45" from the 2x4. Cut eight bottom shelf boards at 26" and five top shelf boards at 50" from the 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets). Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 12 ft 4x4, five 8 ft 2x4 and five 10 ft 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets). Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.
materials: 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Assemble the legs, sides, front/back, and bottom support into a square frame.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf boards with small gaps between them for drainage.
materials: 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top shelf boards the same way, with consistent gaps.
materials: 1x6 cedar boards or fence pickets, 10 ft (or 6 ft pickets)

steps.title: Add the casters and finish
steps.body: Mount caster wheels to the bottom of the legs if using them, then sand and apply exterior stain or sealant.

unresolvedImages.alt: Rolling cedar potting island with slatted shelves
